engineGuide.h2: Volkswagen Derby Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volkswagen Derby across 1977-1984, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.1L Petrol
entries.history: Base 1.1L engine for early Derby L/CL models; robust design but carburettor tuning critical for reliability.
commonFailures: Carburettor wear and tuning issues (typical after 30+ years)
Cooling system leaks from aged hoses/radiator
Valve guide wear on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for HB Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 1.1L Petrol
entries.history: Higher-output 1.1L variant with revised cam profile; fitted to CL/CLS for improved drivability over base HB.
commonFailures: Carburettor jet wear affecting fuel mixture
Head gasket degradation on overheated units
Timing chain tensioner wear above 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for HC Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 1.1L Petrol
entries.history: High-compression 1.1L for Formula E economy model; requires unleaded fuel and is prone to head gasket failure without MLS upgrade.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure due to original composite gasket degradation
Cooling system leaks exacerbating overheating risk
Carburettor tuning sensitivity on ethanol-blended fuels
entries.cta: Get quotes for ACH Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Largest factory petrol engine for Derby; fitted to CLS/GLS for stronger performance while retaining classic carburetted simplicity.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattles on cold start
Cooling system leaks from aged radiator/hoses
Distributor cap brittleness leading to misfires
entries.cta: Get quotes for HH Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Revised 1.3L for late Mk2 Derby; lower compression for improved reliability and emissions compliance in final production years.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (same as HH)
Age-related seal and gasket leaks
Carburettor wear affecting idle quality
entries.cta: Get quotes for HK Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Economy-focused 1.0L introduced for 1981 facelift; less common in UK but valued for fuel efficiency in lightweight Derby chassis.
commonFailures: Carburettor tuning issues on ethanol fuels
Cooling system degradation due to age
Valve seal wear causing oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for GL Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 0.9L Petrol
entries.history: Original 0.9L engine for earliest Derby L models; now rare with most units replaced by 1.1L for better drivability.
commonFailures: Carburettor wear and tuning complexity
Cooling system leaks from aged components
Low power leading to strain on inclines
entries.cta: Get quotes for HA Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Aftermarket diesel conversion engine using Polo/Golf donor; popular for economy but requires upgraded cam followers to prevent premature failure.
commonFailures: Camshaft lobe wear without upgraded followers (OEM 028109309B)
Injector pump calibration drift affecting performance
Glow plug circuit failures in cold weather
entries.cta: Get quotes for AAZ Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Revised AAZ with improved oiling passages; same cam follower upgrade essential for long-term reliability in Derby conversions.
commonFailures: Cam follower wear (same as AAZ; upgrade mandatory)
Turbocharger seal leaks on higher-mileage units
EGR system clogging if fitted in conversion
entries.cta: Get quotes for ADF Volkswagen Derby engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Derby Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volkswagen Derby's 1977-1984 engines are robust but age-related failures are common. The 1.1L ACH petrol and 1.9L AAZ diesel dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the critical failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Volkswagen Derby 1.1L ACH Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Derby CL, Formula E, GLS (1981-1984, ACH 1.1L petrol)
problems.rootCause: Original composite head gaskets degrade with age and heat cycles, allowing coolant/oil mixing and compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, fit MLS gasket upgrade, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block/head undamaged; 20-40k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ACH engine with MLS gasket, upgraded components, bench tested

problems.recommendation: On a Derby valued £2,000-£5,000, a rebuilt engine with MLS gasket upgrade offers better long-term value than repeated head gasket repairs. We recommend replacement if the block shows wear or previous overheating damage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ACH engine prices for your Volkswagen Derby

problems.h4: Camshaft Lobe Wear - Why Volkswagen Derby AAZ Diesel Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Derby CL 1.9D, GLS 1.9D (1982-1984, AAZ/ADF diesel)
problems.rootCause: Original cam followers wear prematurely, causing lobe damage; poor oil maintenance accelerates failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace camshaft and upgraded followers (OEM 028109309B)
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents recurrence if caught early; 30-50k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt AAZ engine with upgraded cam followers, bench tested

problems.recommendation: AAZ engines with confirmed cam wear should be replaced, not repaired - the cost of head removal often approaches a rebuilt unit. If caught early (noise only), upgraded followers may suffice.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AAZ Volkswagen Derby diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Cooling System Failure - Why Volkswagen Derby Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: All Volkswagen Derby variants (1977-1984, all engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Age-related (40+ years); hoses/radiators perish regardless of mileage
problems.rootCause: Rubber hoses, plastic radiator tanks and water pumps degrade with age; Derby's longer wheelbase reduces airflow vs Polo.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hoses, radiator, thermostat, water pump, flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; extends engine life significantly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after head gasket/warping from overheating
repairOptions.longevity: Required if overheating caused internal damage

problems.recommendation: Proactively replace all cooling components on any Derby over 30 years old - it's cheaper than an engine replacement. If overheating has occurred, inspect for head damage before deciding on repair vs replace.
problems.cta: Compare Volkswagen Derby cooling system specialist prices

problems.h4: Carburettor Issues - Why Early Volkswagen Derby Petrol Engines Run Poorly
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Derby L, CL, CLS (1977-1981, HB/HC/HH carburetted engines)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Age-related; carburettors wear regardless of mileage
problems.rootCause: Solex carburettors develop vacuum leaks, worn jets and sticking chokes; ethanol fuel accelerates degradation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean, replace seals/jets, bench test, refit and tune
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise sound; requires specialist knowledge

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new/rebuilt carburettor, upgraded seals
repairOptions.longevity: Better value if engine has other age-related wear

problems.recommendation: If the engine block is sound, a carburettor rebuild by a classic VW specialist is cost-effective. If the engine also shows compression loss or oil consumption, replacement is the smarter investment.
problems.cta: Find Volkswagen Derby carburettor specialists UK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Derby
finalCta.paragraph: The Volkswagen Derby's classic status makes preservation worthwhile, but age-related failures require smart decisions. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand Derby-Polo interchangeability, offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components that address known weak points. Compare quotes, verify warranty terms, and get back on the road without main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Derby Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Derby and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Volkswagen Derby 0.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The smallest engine offered in the Derby, the 0.9L HA was fitted to early L models (1977-1979) and is now rare; most owners upgrade to 1.1L units for better drivability while retaining classic character.
commonFailurePoints: cooling system degradation due to age

items.title: Volkswagen Derby 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced in 1981 for economy-focused variants, the 1.0L GL engine offered improved fuel consumption over the 1.1L while maintaining adequate performance for the lightweight Derby body.

items.title: Volkswagen Derby 1.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common Derby engine, the 1.1L family (HB, HC, ACH) powered L, CL and the unique Formula E variant; ACH codes are prone to head gasket failure requiring MLS upgrade.

items.title: Volkswagen Derby 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The largest petrol option, the 1.3L family (HH, HJ, HK, HW, DX) offered stronger performance for CLS and GLS variants; timing chain tensioner wear is the primary concern on higher-mileage units.

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Derby Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen Derby's 1977-1984 production, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Derby launched with Polo-derived petrol engines; the 0.9L HA was quickly superseded by the more popular 1.1L units.
keyChanges: Derby nameplate introduced on Polo-based saloon body
HA 0.9L and HB 1.1L engines offered at launch
Rectangular headlights and Derby-specific rear quarters differentiate from Polo
knownFor: Strong parts sharing with Mk1 Polo simplifies repairs
Carburettor tuning critical for smooth running
Cooling system upgrades recommended for all surviving examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 1977 Volkswagen Derby replacement engines

years.description: The 1981 facelift introduced the economy-focused Formula E variant with high-compression ACH engine requiring unleaded fuel.
keyChanges: Formula E variant launched with high-compression 1.1L ACH engine
Revised interior trim and badging for facelift models
HH 1.3L engine offered in CLS for stronger performance
knownFor: Formula E requires unleaded fuel; pinking on regular fuel causes damage
ACH engines prone to head gasket failure without MLS upgrade
Polo Mk1 donor engines remain direct-fit replacements
years.ticker: Volkswagen Derby Formula E engine replacement • ACH head gasket failure • Polo engine fits Derby
enquiries: Volkswagen Derby Formula E engine replacement

years.description: The Mk2 Derby introduced round headlights and revised trim; aftermarket 1.9L diesel conversions using AAZ/ADF engines became popular for economy.
keyChanges: Mk2 facelift with round headlights and updated interior
GLS variant introduced with 1.3L HK engine
Aftermarket AAZ/ADF diesel conversions offered for CL/GLS chassis
knownFor: AAZ diesel conversions require upgraded cam followers (028109309B)
Mk2 Derby shares more components with Polo Mk2 (1981-1994)
Final production year 1984; rebadged as Polo Classic thereafter
years.cta: Get quotes for 1984 Volkswagen Derby replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Derby AAZ camshaft wear • GLS 1.3L timing chain • Derby becomes Polo Classic 1985

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Volkswagen Derby was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Volkswagen Derby specialists.

faq.h2: Volkswagen Derby Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Can I put a Polo engine in my Volkswagen Derby?
items.answer: Yes - Mk1 and early Mk2 Polo 1.1L/1.3L petrol engines are direct replacements for equivalent Derby units, significantly expanding available donor options for classic restoration projects.
keyPoints: Polo Mk1 (1975-1981) engines share identical mounting points
Engine codes HB, HC, HH, HJ, HK are interchangeable
Verify carburettor linkage and exhaust manifold compatibility
Diesel AAZ conversions require custom fabrication regardless of donor
items.cta: Compare Polo-sourced Volkswagen Derby engine prices

items.question: What causes head gasket failure on Volkswagen Derby 1.1L ACH engines?
items.answer: Original composite head gaskets degrade with age and heat cycles; upgrading to a multi-layer steel (MLS) gasket during rebuild prevents recurrence on Formula E and CL models.
keyPoints: ACH engines (1981-1984) most affected due to higher compression
Overheating from cooling system leaks accelerates failure
MLS gasket upgrade adds £75-£150 to rebuild cost but prevents repeat failure
Compression test before purchase identifies at-risk units
items.cta: Get quotes for ACH Volkswagen Derby engine with MLS gasket

items.question: Is the Volkswagen Derby AAZ diesel engine reliable?
items.answer: The AAZ conversion offers excellent economy but requires upgraded cam followers (OEM 028109309B) to prevent premature camshaft lobe wear - a known weak point on high-mileage units.
keyPoints: Cam follower upgrade is essential, not optional, for long-term reliability
Injector pump calibration requires specialist knowledge
Glow plug circuit failures common in cold weather
Rebuilt AAZ units with upgraded followers cost £1,100-£2,200 supply-only
items.cta: Find Volkswagen Derby AAZ diesel specialists UK

items.question: How much does a Volkswagen Derby engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Volkswagen Derby engines range from £895 (1.1L petrol) to £2,300 (1.9L diesel) supply-only; supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on workshop location and vehicle condition.
keyPoints: Used engines from Polo donors start around £350-£1,450
Reconditioned units with warranty offer best value for restoration
Diesel conversions cost more due to specialist fitting requirements
Get multiple quotes via our comparison tool to secure best price
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Derby engine replacement quotes UK

items.question: What is the most reliable Volkswagen Derby engine?
items.answer: The 1.3L HH/HK petrol engines offer the best balance of reliability, parts availability and performance for classic Derby ownership, with fewer inherent weaknesses than the 1.1L ACH or diesel conversions.
keyPoints: HH/HK engines robust if cooling system maintained proactively
Timing chain tensioner replacement recommended at 100k miles
Carburettor tuning simpler than fuel injection on late DX units
Polo Mk1/Mk2 donor availability keeps parts costs reasonable

items.question: Does Volkswagen still make replacement engines for the Derby?
items.answer: No - Volkswagen discontinued all new Derby engine components in March 1996; all replacements now come from specialist rebuilders, breakers or compatible Polo donor vehicles.
keyPoints: No new OEM engines available since 1996 discontinuation
Specialist rebuilders use upgraded components addressing known failures
Used engines from Polo donors remain the most affordable option
Verify warranty terms when purchasing rebuilt/reconditioned units
items.cta: Find Volkswagen Derby engine specialists with warranty

items.question: How long does a Volkswagen Derby engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch in 2-4 working days; supply & fit services require 3-7 days depending on workshop workload and parts availability for your specific engine code.
keyPoints: Used engines may require sourcing time if not in stock
Rebuilt units built to order take 2-3 weeks typical turnaround
Supply & fit includes removal, fitting, fluids and road test
Book early during peak classic car season (spring/summer)
items.cta: Check Volkswagen Derby engine delivery times UK

items.question: Can I convert my Volkswagen Derby to diesel?
items.answer: Yes - aftermarket AAZ/ADF 1.9L diesel conversions are possible using Polo/Golf donor engines, but require custom mounts, exhaust and fuel system modifications by a specialist.
keyPoints: Conversion cost typically £2,500-£4,500 including engine and labour
AAZ cam follower upgrade (028109309B) is mandatory for reliability
Verify conversion quality and documentation before purchasing
Petrol engines remain simpler and more affordable to replace
items.cta: Enquire about Volkswagen Derby diesel conversion specialists

items.question: Where do I find the engine code on my Volkswagen Derby?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the engine block near the distributor housing on most Derby engines; also recorded in the vehicle service book and on the VIN plate under the bonnet.
keyPoints: Clean the area near the distributor to read the stamped code
Common codes: HB, HC, HH, ACH, HK, AAZ
DVLA registration check confirms variant but not engine code
Photograph the stamp when sourcing replacement parts
items.cta: Identify your Volkswagen Derby engine code instantly

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding my Volkswagen Derby engine or replacing it?
items.answer: For Derbys valued £2,000-£5,000, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components often offers better long-term value than repeated repairs, especially if the block shows wear or previous overheating damage.
keyPoints: Rebuild costs approach replacement price on heavily worn units
Rebuilt engines include warranty and upgraded failure-point components
Used Polo donor engines offer budget option for running projects
Get quotes for both options before deciding
items.cta: Compare rebuild vs replacement costs for your Volkswagen Derby
